title: strychnos myrioneura
intro: Strychnos myrioneura, also known as the African poison nut, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of the toxic alkaloids strychnine and brucine. Traditionally, the seeds of Strychnos myrioneura have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often extracted and used as a powder or tincture, although caution is advised due to the toxic nature of the alkaloids.
